rationale_for_baseline_tier: A large, mature, fluctuant, pointing cutaneous abscess with surrounding cellulitis and low-grade systemic features (chills, malaise) in a systemically STABLE patient (measured Temp 38.2 C, HR 95, BP 130/80, SpO2 99% — no shock, no airway involvement). The definitive treatment is incision and drainage, which telehealth cannot provide, and the cellulitis needs oral antibiotics — so this requires URGENT same-day in-person assessment (T3, urgent care / same-day GP). It is not T2 (a pointing abscess with spreading cellulitis and systemic upset should not wait 24-48 hours) and not T4/T5 (the patient is haemodynamically stable with no sepsis or airway compromise, so an emergency ambulance would be over-triage). The negative deep neck-space/airway screen is what keeps it at T3 rather than T5.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Redness spreading rapidly (e.g. tracking down the back), fever above 39 C, rigors, feeling faint or dizzy, or inability to keep fluids down
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Present to an emergency department the same day, without waiting for the booked drainage
escalation_edges.rationale: These indicate the infection is becoming systemic (evolving sepsis) or the cellulitis is spreading uncontrolled — needing IV antibiotics and inpatient assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads quickly, your fever climbs over 39, you get the shakes/rigors, feel faint, or can't keep fluids down, go to the nearest emergency department straight away — don't wait for the drainage appointment.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Confusion, drowsiness, or signs of shock (cold clammy skin, racing heart, feeling you might collapse)
escalation_edges.rationale: Confusion or shock signals severe sepsis from the soft-tissue infection — an immediate life threat requiring emergency ambulance and resuscitation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you become confused or drowsy, or feel like you might pass out with a racing heart and cold sweaty skin, call 000 for an ambulance right away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New difficulty swallowing, drooling, a muffled or changed voice, trouble opening the mouth, or any difficulty breathing
escalation_edges.rationale: These would indicate the neck infection has extended into a deep neck space with airway threat — a time-critical emergency that can obstruct the airway within minutes.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you start to have trouble swallowing, drool, notice your voice change or muffle, can't open your mouth, or have any trouble breathing, call 000 immediately — this could mean the infection is spreading deeper in your neck.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Baseline disposition: a mature pointing fluctuant abscess with surrounding cellulitis and systemic upset that telehealth cannot drain
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment for incision and drainage plus antibiotics
escalation_edges.rationale: The abscess needs same-day drainage that cannot be delivered remotely; this is the correct baseline action even if no further deterioration occurs.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This lump is a pocket of pus that needs to be drained today by a clinician in person — I'm arranging an urgent same-day appointment, and you'll be started on antibiotics for the surrounding redness.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with no one available to check on them overnight
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold to seek help and make the same-day in-person visit happen today rather than risk overnight deterioration alone; advise the patient to tell a friend/neighbour or keep a phone to hand, and reinforce the 000 triggers clearly given no one is present to notice deterioration.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a boil that has turned into a pocket of pus (an abscess) with spreading redness and is making you feel unwell — it needs to be drained today by a clinician in person and you need antibiotics for the surrounding redness. I'm arranging an urgent same-day appointment. Do not squeeze or pop it yourself. Take the antibiotics as prescribed, use warm compresses, and keep the area clean.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day in-person appointment (GP or urgent care clinic); call 000 if emergency features develop
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If it is after hours and no same-day appointment is available, attend an after-hours GP or urgent care clinic today; if the emergency features below develop at any time, go to the emergency department or call 000.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth impression: large cervical furuncle/cutaneous abscess with perilesional cellulitis and low-grade systemic features, systemically stable, deep neck-space/airway screen negative. Referred same-day for incision and drainage and commenced on oral flucloxacillin; wound review in 48 hours. Consider pus swab MC&S at drainage.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the lesion as self-care or a routine GP problem — misses that a mature abscess needs same-day drainage and that cellulitis with systemic features needs antibiotics.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The abscess enlarges and ruptures uncontrolled, the cellulitis spreads, and the patient — alone overnight — progresses to systemic sepsis before being seen.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Deferring to a prompt GP review in 24-48 hours rather than same-day — too slow for a pointing abscess with spreading cellulitis and systemic upset.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending a systemically stable patient with no sepsis or airway features to the emergency department or by 000 ambulance, when urgent same-day in-person drainage is the appropriate pathway.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot palpate fluctuance, measure the true extent of cellulitis, or fully exclude early deep-space extension.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the visible pointing lesion plus spreading erythema and systemic upset as a drainable abscess with cellulitis, arrange same-day in-person assessment, and safety-net firmly for sepsis and airway/deep-space features.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure and default to home self-care because it 'looks like just a boil', or conversely dispatch an ambulance for a stable patient.
